name: research
description: Multi-provider web research: searches DuckDuckGo/Wikipedia (plus Brave/Tavily/Google when keys are set), fetches pages, builds solution-oriented research dossiers with corroborated sources.

name: homebuyer
description: California first-time home-buyer advisor: explainers (roles, timeline, terms, house vs condo, loan types), transparent calculators (affordability, monthly cost, closing costs, cash to close, rent vs buy), and research-verifiable live reference data (mortgage rates, Riverside County loan limits, California Dream For All, insurance market, Murrieta/Temecula prices, Prop 13/Mello-Roos). Deterministic and offline; cross-check live numbers against sources via the research asset.

name: nestegg
description: Investing education in plain words a kid can follow: every vehicle explained with honest odds (stocks, bonds, index funds, 401k/IRA/Roth/HSA, crypto, calls/puts, Kalshi, gold, wine), the order-of-operations ladder (step by step), tax rules incl. California, insider reads on brokers/advisors/influencers, money-trap red flags, and calculators (compound growth, fee drag, employer match, goal timeline). Research-verifiable reference data (2026 contribution limits, rates).

name: lawguide
description: California + federal legal information (NOT legal advice, not confidential): know-your-rights, which-arena (criminal/civil/traffic/administrative/immigration), how-a-lawyer-would-handle-it playbooks (arrested, sued, accused, contract, landlord, car accident, debt, immigration stop, IRS), the players' mindsets (prosecutor/judge/police/detective/PI/clerk/public defender/ICE/IRS), red-flag traps (talking to police, missing court, notario fraud), find-a-lawyer incl. free options, and a live CA/Riverside/LA/San Diego legal-resource finder. Verifiable reference data (small-claims limit, statutes of limitation, deadlines). Always routes serious matters to a licensed lawyer.

name: jobhunt
description: California job-search coaching: career discovery (what job fits you, RIASEC-based) + career pathways (how to get from A to B, e.g. tech support→cybersecurity, retail→manager, CNA→RN), the hiring funnel, beat-the-ATS keyword strategy, resume/interview tips, how recruiters/hiring managers/ATS/HR/references think, the 'why am I stuck?' diagnostic (no responses/interviews/offers/low pay/no direction → broken gate + fixes), salary negotiation, and California living-wage + highest-to-lowest-paying job-market data. Personalizes to a saved candidate profile; pulls live pay/living-wage/cert data via research (BLS/O*NET/CA EDD/MIT Living Wage). Coaching with real data, not job guarantees.

name: polymath
description: The expert-in-every-position consultant: ~70 technical job titles across 8 practice families — (1) AI Engineering & Ops incl. research (RL/reinforcement, pre-training, Applied AI enterprise, Technical Specialist–Claude, Knowledge team, Claude Platform enablement, Human Data Interface), (2) Data & BI incl. Looker/Tableau + data operations + detection-and-response data science, (3) Cloud & Infrastructure incl. network, capacity & efficiency, performance, (4) Security/Trust/Forensics incl. detection & response, incident response manager, identity & access controls (IAM), cybersecurity products, security software engineering, (5) Systems & Technical Support incl. staff software engineer + developer productivity, (6) Leadership & Delivery incl. research operations, (7) AI Safety & Frontier Threat — red-team engineering, cyber & CBRNE threat investigation, safeguards policy (incl. scams/fraud), AI safety program management, (8) Product Design & Frontend — design engineer, web/UX. THE FRONT DOOR: ask_the_expert takes ANY question — troubleshooting, work problems, improvements, pitches — and answers how the right specialist would: their method step-by-step, the diagnostic questions they'd ask you, their tools, plus research queries to verify current specifics; expert_verdict folds research findings back into the final playbook. set_context saves your role/stacks/constraints so every answer personalizes. Also: build_it/finalize_build (project plans), level_up (career gaps), day_in_the_life (domain maps), how_its_done (US/UK/France/Canada/Mexico/South America/Japan), and the research verify loop for every live fact.

name: healthguide
description: Health information & navigation MCP with a non-suppressible emergency/crisis override (911/988) that runs first on every tool, regardless of what was asked. Specialist routing incl. marriage/family/parent-child counseling and sports trainers, SOCRATES-style root-cause diagnostic questions, multi-country research-verified evidence-checking for health/nutrition/aging claims (NIH, Japan MHLW, EFSA, Health Canada, WHO, Cochrane), macronutrient + insulin + sports-nutrition science, weight-training/calisthenics/military-calisthenics methodology, a crisis-gated hope tool for feeling stuck (real clinical next-steps, never a dead end), medical misinformation red flags, care navigation (insurance/records/HIPAA), and appointment prep. Information and navigation only -- never diagnosis or treatment.

name: overseer
description: Observability MCP for this orchestrator: replay_case renders one case's full timeline (routing, every asset call, arguments, result/error) — the "what did the agent system actually do" answer. audit_report gives the system-health snapshot: calls/errors per asset, and warns if an asset was called but never registered. list_cases_brief finds a case_id to replay. Reads any orchestrator's cases.json/registry.json by path, not tied to this one deployment.

name: curiosity
description: Science & curiosity explainer across physics/quantum, space/astronomy, life/biology, earth/geology/geography, ancient history/archaeology, great scientific minds (Einstein/Tesla/Newton/Darwin/Curie), and computing/AI. explore (any topic → the field, the big questions, touchstones, rabbit holes), myth_vs_reality, go_deeper, how_we_know (the methods behind knowledge), and a rigorous check_claim → claim_verdict research loop that separates real science from pseudoscience (ancient aliens, free energy, suppressed-genius myths) with honest evidence tiers. Curiosity with honesty; everything checkable routes through research.

name: education
description: General education across high school, college, and university: the subjects and their actual classes (mathematics, natural sciences, English/language arts, social studies, world languages, arts, computer science & tech, career-technical/vocational, health & PE), the course ladders (what to take before what, e.g. Algebra→Geometry→Trig→Pre-Calc→Calculus), evidence-based study skills (active recall, spaced repetition), and graduation/degree/admissions requirements (structure explained; specifics verified live via research since they vary by state/school). Maps the education and points to curiosity/research for the actual course content.

name: communication
description: Communication & reading people: how to speak in public (speeches, presentations), at work (meetings, email, presenting to leadership, hard conversations), and at home (family, partners, conflict); debate & persuasion (ethos/pathos/logos, rebuttal); listening & steelmanning the other side; spotting faulty arguments and logical fallacies (being hard to fool); and reading people HONESTLY like an interrogator — baseline-and-deviation, clusters, mismatches, funnel questions, silence, following breadcrumbs — with the firm, evidence-based caveat that body language is NOT a lie detector and the '93% body language' claim is a myth. Real technique plus honesty, not mind-reading tricks. prepare gives per-situation playbooks; steelman, spot_fallacies, read_people, and myth_vs_reality go deep.

name: sports
description: Sports, centered on soccer (football) with other major sports covered lighter (basketball, American football, baseball, tennis, rugby, cricket): how each game works — rules, positions, formations, tactics — and, most of all, how TALENT is identified. The four-corners scouting model (technical, tactical, physical, psychological), the concrete markers scouts trust (scanning before receiving, first touch, decision speed, off-ball movement, composure), the development pathway (play constantly → academy → trials → get seen), and the honest caveats (relative age effect, late bloomers, the tiny pro odds — develop for love + a plan B). Current teams/scores/transfers/local academies route to research.

name: government
description: World governments and immigration: how each region is governed (United States, Europe/EU+UK, Middle East, Japan, Russia, South America) and the practical path to move between them — residency and immigration routes, employment permits (H-1B, EU Blue Card, UK Skilled Worker, Gulf kafala sponsorship, Mercosur), and travel/entry visas (Schengen 90/180, ESTA, e-visas). Explains the stable structure; routes current specifics to research and official government sources; general information only, never legal advice; defers serious cases to a licensed immigration lawyer. Complements lawguide (legal rights and US situations).

name: linguistics
description: Human languages and how they work: the major language families (Indo-European, Sino-Tibetan, Afro-Asiatic, Niger-Congo, Austronesian, Dravidian, Japonic/Koreanic/isolates) and where they're spoken, how language itself works (sounds/phonetics, word-building/morphology, word order/syntax, writing systems, how languages change), the evidence-based science of learning one (comprehensible input, high-frequency words, spaced repetition, honest hours-to-fluency), and the myths (no language is 'primitive', adults CAN learn, bilingualism doesn't confuse kids). Current speaker counts route to research. Complements education's language classes.

name: faiths
description: World religions and faiths, explained evenhandedly from the outside: what the major traditions actually believe and practice — Christianity, Islam, Judaism (including Kabbalah, its mystical tradition), Buddhism, Hinduism, Sikhism, plus Taoism, Confucianism, Shinto, Jainism, Bahá'í and secular worldviews — their origins, core beliefs, practices, texts, and branches, and how they relate (the Abrahamic and Dharmic families, shared roots, real differences, the common Golden Rule thread). Neutral and respectful; never proselytizes, ranks, or gives spiritual direction. Historical depth routes to curiosity/research.

name: loop
description: Loop Engineering: how to design, build, debug, and honestly evaluate agentic AI loops — the observe→think→act→reflect agent loop and its patterns (the basic agent loop, ReAct, plan-and-execute, reflexion/self-correction, the tool-use loop, RAG loops, multi-agent orchestration, evaluator-optimizer, human-in-the-loop, memory/state). explain_pattern maps the loop patterns; design_loop picks an architecture for what you want an agent to do; building_blocks covers the six things needed to make a loop AUTONOMOUS (Connectors/MCP collection, Automations, Skills, Subagents/maker≠checker, Memory, Worktrees); model_requirements covers what the model must do well (cheap tokens, robust tool-calling/JSON, concurrency); debug_loop diagnoses a loop that never stops, thrashes tools, hallucinates tool calls, drifts off task, or blows the context window; eval_loop shows how to measure an agent honestly (consistency ≠ correctness, golden sets, trajectory, LLM-as-judge caveats); myth_vs_reality debunks the folklore ('more agents = better', 'just add reflection', temperature-0 determinism); check_practice → practice_verdict verify fast-moving framework/technique specifics via research. The engineering is in the loop around the model, not the model.

name: openai
description: OpenAI Platform Engineer — complete expert on ChatGPT and Codex, plus the API beneath them. ChatGPT the product: the Goal/Context/Output/Boundaries prompt shape, Chat vs Work mode, Projects, Plugins (Drive/Gmail/Slack/GitHub), Personalization, custom instructions, attachments, steering vs queuing. Codex the agentic coding surface: gpt-5.3-codex, the open-source Rust CLI, the IDE extension, cloud, PR code review, reasoning effort, compaction, apply_patch, /plan /goal /review, @codex review, the sandbox and approval policy, and AGENTS.md — the durable repo context file (nested; closest to the edited file wins), which is the highest-leverage thing you write for Codex. Also the API primitives: Responses (own the loop), Agents SDK (SDK runs it), Chat Completions, hosted tools + remote MCP, realtime/voice, embeddings, fine-tuning, batch and prompt caching. how_they_use_it is the profession lens — software engineer, systems analyst, researcher, IT admin, lawyer, realtor, clinician, accountant, teacher, marketer, manager, student, writer, product designer — each with the trap that burns them: fabricated citations, Fair Housing language, PHI, wrong-question SQL, invented packages. pick_primitive names the layer for a project. debug_openai diagnoses breakage. migration_check audits Chat Completions, which is NOT deprecated. myth_vs_reality debunks folklore. check_openai then openai_verdict verify version-sensitive facts (model IDs, pricing, limits) via research, labeled VERIFIED/UPDATED/UNVERIFIED — never recalled from memory. how_they_build maps the 19 engineering, research, safety, and operations roles that build and run OpenAI — from Forward Deployed Engineer and Agent Post-Training to Threat Investigator, Workday Engineer, and Strategy and Operations — each with what they do, their stack, and the trap outsiders get wrong. Deterministic and offline.

name: aiforge
description: The AI/ML engineering craft — the hands-on plumbing beneath any AI app, agentic or not. Four expert lenses: Python for AI (NumPy, pandas, PyTorch, environments, performance, the traps); foundation-model integration (inference options hosted vs self-hosted vs local, structured output, cost/latency, offline evals, guardrails); Hugging Face and LangChain as CODE (Transformers and the Hub, PEFT/LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning with TRL, serving and quantization with vLLM, LCEL and create_agent, RAG retrieval plumbing); and LLM/NLP fundamentals (tokenization, embeddings, transformer architecture, fine-tune vs RAG vs prompt, decoding and sampling, classic NLP). explain_topic is the front door; build_it recommends the simplest approach and stack for a task; debug maps a symptom to cause and fix; myth_vs_reality debunks the folklore; check_practice then practice_verdict verify fast-moving framework specifics via research. Owns the CRAFT of building — defers agent architecture (ReAct, when to loop, multi-agent) to loop and OpenAI-platform specifics to openai. Deterministic and offline.

name: gitforge
description: Git and GitHub as a craft. Git the tool: the three trees, branching, merge vs rebase, undo and recovery (reflog, reset, revert, restore), history archaeology (log, blame, bisect), conflicts, stash, the object model, and team branching strategies. GitHub the platform: repos, forks, pull requests and review, Actions CI/CD, releases, tags, issues, the REST and GraphQL API, the gh CLI, and repository security (branch protection, secret scanning, Dependabot). explain_topic is the front door; how_to gives the exact commands for a goal; debug maps a symptom to its fix; myth_vs_reality debunks the folklore; check_practice and practice_verdict verify the GitHub surface. Recovery is the point: committed history is almost never truly gone. Deterministic and offline.

name: promptcraft
description: Prompt engineering as a vendor-neutral craft: reliable output from any LLM. Techniques (zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought and when to skip it for reasoning models, self-consistency, role and persona, decomposition, ReAct, tree-of-thought, meta), structure (the anatomy of a strong instruction, XML-tag and delimiter framing, output formatting, context and example selection), and reliability (iterating and evaluating on a golden set, anti-patterns, robustness and injection, the reasoning-model caveat). explain_topic is the front door; build_prompt scaffolds one for a goal; improve_prompt critiques a draft; myth_vs_reality debunks the folklore; check_practice and practice_verdict verify model-specific guidance. Owns technique; defers the OpenAI and ChatGPT shape to openai and the build decision to aiforge. Deterministic and offline.

name: apiforge
description: AI APIs and Postman: calling, checking, and automating APIs, focused on AI and LLM endpoints. Fundamentals (HTTP and REST, status codes, auth with keys/bearer/OAuth, rate limits and retries, pagination, JSON payloads and headers), Postman as the workbench (collections, environments and secret variables, assertions and pre-request scripts, mocks and monitors, the CLI and Newman for CI, the 2026 AI features), and AI endpoints (request and response shape, streaming and SSE, reliability with retries and timeouts and cost, verifying non-deterministic responses by properties not exact strings, webhooks and async jobs). explain_topic is the front door; how_to sets up a call or check; debug maps an HTTP symptom to its fix; myth_vs_reality debunks the folklore; check_practice and practice_verdict verify provider specs and Postman features. Two rules: credentials never in the URL, and AI endpoints verified by properties. Deterministic and offline.

name: kalshi
description: Event contracts and prediction markets (Kalshi, Polymarket): binary one-dollar settlement, the series/event/market structure, order book, maker and taker fees, implied probability, breakeven after fees, position sizing, CFTC regulation and the unsettled state-law question, sportsbook comparison, taxes, and the free public market-data API. Holds no prices, no fee numbers, and no legal status — those move and route through research.

name: elevenlabs
description: ElevenLabs voice tools: text-to-speech (natural narration and voiceover), speech-to-text transcription, and voice generation/cloning. Turns text into spoken audio and audio into text.

name: docingest
description: Document ingestion: retrieve authorized files from a URL, a webpage/Confluence page (with attachment discovery), or connector bytes; detect type by signature; extract text/tables from PDF (OCR-flag for scans), Word/Excel/PowerPoint, CSV/HTML/Markdown/email; return concise, cited, size-limited results. Redacts credentials and neutralizes prompt injection; never bypasses logins.

name: ghmonitor
description: GitHub delivery monitoring: pass/fail/pending status for commits and pull requests (Actions workflows, required checks, commit statuses, deployments, code scanning), with links; diagnoses a failing run from its redacted logs (what failed, likely cause, impacted files, owner, next step); dedupes to state-changes only. Authorized read-only; never exposes tokens.

name: docsearch
description: Hybrid search over previously-ingested content (docs, pages, Confluence, PDFs, spreadsheets, slides, CI records): full-text BM25 for exact phrases/names/IDs/error codes/filenames/code symbols, plus semantic vector search, fused by hybrid ranking with metadata filters and recency. Enforces query-time authorization (never returns content outside your scopes) and returns concise cited excerpts, not whole documents. index_document/delete_source/search/search_stats.
